title: cerasus microlepis
intro: Cerasus microlepis, also known as the Japanese apricot or Japanese cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Cerasus microlepis include the potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
